factors.name: US-Iran Ceasefire Deal Progress
factors.summary: Trump administration reports Iran talks 'proceeding nicely' with plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz in ~30 days and extend ceasefire 60 days. This is a risk-on catalyst that typically favors equities and weakens safe-haven assets like USD.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude down ~7% to test $90/bbl support on Hormuz reopening hopes. Lower energy costs reduce inflation pressure and support consumer spending and corporate margins, positive for growth equities.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: DXY dropped to 2-week lows on geopolitical de-escalation optimism and rate-cut expectations under new Fed Chair Warsh. Weaker dollar typically favors tech (high international revenue exposure) and reduces headwind from FX translation.

factors.name: Real Yield Risk / Higher-for-Longer Narrative
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Treasury curve flashing 'higher-for-longer' warning under Warsh; 10Y yield spread tightening to 1-year lows. Mega-cap tech and NDX are highly sensitive to real yields; sustained higher-for-longer would compress growth multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical Escalation Risk (Underlying Conflict)
factors.summary: Despite optimistic headlines, US forces conducted 'self-defense strikes' on Iranian targets near Hormuz; explosions reported in Bandar Abbas, Sirik, Jask. Israel-Lebanon hostilities also escalating. Deal remains fragile; de-escalation narrative could reverse sharply if talks break.

factors.name: Market Momentum / Position Unwinding
factors.summary: Light holiday volumes (US Memorial Day, Europe closed) and futures surged on risk-on sentiment. High velocity moves on thin liquidity often revert; NDX likely chopping into Tuesday's data and earnings calendar.

factors.name: Earnings Season & Forward Guidance Vacuum
factors.summary: No mega-cap tech earnings or hyperscaler capex guidance in recent news window. AI demand narrative intact but no fresh catalysts; awaits NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings and cloud capex outlook for conviction.

factors.name: Broad S&P 500 Earnings Breadth Improvement
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports S&P 500 profit growth fastest in nearly 5 years, with Magnificent 7 dominance fading as non-mega-cap names pulling weight. Broadening earnings support could underpin NDX resilience.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty (Warsh Era)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh as new Fed Chair signals potential hawkish tilt; market pricing excess BOK hikes and BoJ intervention fears. Uncertainty on US rates trajectory creates headwind for 2025-2026 growth multiple re-rating.

factors.name: China-Related Tariffs Eased but Trust Eroded
factors.summary: CNBC reports tariffs eased but trust in US-China relations remains damaged. No major new trade escalation, but lack of confidence limits upside for China-sensitive tech (TSMC, semiconductor equities).

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between risk-on euphoria from US-Iran ceasefire hopes and geopolitical fragility on the ground (active US military strikes, Israel-Lebanon escalation). Oil collapse and USD weakness are classically bullish for growth tech, but the 'higher-for-longer' Fed narrative under Warsh threatens long-duration multiple expansion. Markets rallied on thin Memorial Day holiday liquidity; move is in Phase 2 (underway, not extended) but vulnerable to reversal if deal talks falter or real yields spike. Earnings catalyst vacuum and uncertainty on Fed trajectory keep conviction capped.
catalysts_ahead: US Consumer Confidence (Tuesday, per market calendar)
Earnings reports: Elbit Systems, Zscaler, Semtech (Tuesday)
Treasury curve dynamics / 10Y yield move on inflation expectations
FOMC meeting timing (date TBD in next weeks)
CPI/PCE data (TBD, high-impact for real yield re-pricing)
Mega-cap tech earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA — dates not in this window)
screening.risk_warning: Deal collapse or fresh Iran escalation (military strikes widen, Strait re-closes) would reverse risk-on bias instantly. Alternatively, if Warsh signals hawkish pivot or real yields spike on fresh inflation data, higher-for-longer narrative crushes NDX growth multiple. Light liquidity on holiday week means stops can be run; reversion likely if sentiment cracks.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended on 4h (already down -0.58% in 4h despite overnight euphoria), advise waiting for retest of 29700 resistance or pullback to 29300-29400 support before fresh entry. Risk-on trades safer on confirmation of deal progress + earnings upside; avoid chasing into thin holiday volumes.
